mandel-charles.text: As of 2026-07-04, FEC filings show Charles Mandel's campaign has raised $1,002,821 and spent $758,563.
No U.S. House roll-call voting record was in our data set for Charles Mandel (non-incumbent candidates have no floor votes to record). Public candidate filings are listed at the source below.

gonzalez-vicente.text: As of 2026-07-04, FEC filings show Vicente Gonzalez's campaign has raised $2,907,549 and spent $1,293,623.

On H R 1 (22-May-2025), Vicente Gonzalez voted Nay. A Yea vote passed the 2025 budget reconciliation package extending the 2017 tax cuts, adding new tax breaks (e.g. on tips and overtime pay), cutting projected Medicaid and SNAP spending, boosting border and defense funding, and raising the debt limit.

On H R 29 (7-Jan-2025), Vicente Gonzalez voted Yea. A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

base.summary: TX-34: 2 candidate(s) on file for U.S. House of Representatives, TX-34. Bullets below summarize each candidate's FEC-reported campaign finance and, for sitting U.S. House members, selected 2025-2026 roll-call votes on file. This is comparative, sourced information only -- it does not recommend a choice.
base.caveats: Only facts present in our sourced candidate data set are shown; missing fields mean no public data was on file at build time. Voting records only exist for sitting U.S. House incumbents; non-incumbent challengers have no federal roll-call history.

now.text: No recorded evidence in our set to project from for Charles Mandel.

Vicente Gonzalez already voted Yea on H R 29 (7-Jan-2025). A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions. That's already decided for the current term.

long_term.text: If Vicente Gonzalez's vote on H R 29 reflects how they would vote on similar bills in the future, that pattern would likely keep shaping this issue over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Vicente Gonzalez keeps voting the same way on similar bills in future terms.

gonzalez-vicente.text: On H R 6703 (17-Dec-2025), Vicente Gonzalez voted Nay. A Yea vote passed a Republican-backed alternative to extending the enhanced ACA marketplace subsidies, restructuring health-insurance premium subsidy rules for individual-market plans.
On H R 498 (18-Dec-2025), Vicente Gonzalez voted Yea. A Yea vote barred state Medicaid programs from using federal Medicaid funds to cover gender-transition procedures for minors.

gonzalez-vicente.text: On H R 26 (7-Feb-2025), Vicente Gonzalez voted Yea. A Yea vote barred the President from declaring or enforcing a moratorium on hydraulic fracturing (fracking) for oil and gas without prior congressional approval.

On H R 7744 (5-Mar-2026), Vicente Gonzalez voted Nay. A Yea vote passed the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, funding DHS (including CBP and ICE border security and immigration enforcement operations) for the fiscal year.
